content.text: What is an Azure AD B2C Tenant?

Azure Active Directory B2C (Business-to-Consumer) is a customer identity and access management (CIAM) service built on the same infrastructure as Azure AD but deployed as a completely separate tenant dedicated to consumer-facing applications. Unlike your workforce (work) tenant, a B2C tenant is purpose-built for external customers — people with personal email addresses, social accounts, or no pre-existing Microsoft identity.

Important: Azure AD B2C is a legacy product. Microsoft's current CIAM platform is Microsoft Entra External ID, which replaces B2C for new projects. However, B2C remains supported for existing deployments and offers deeper custom-policy flexibility (Identity Experience Framework) that External ID does not yet match.
Anonymous customers, social logins, local accounts
Isolated — no access to Microsoft 365 or Intune
Deep UI, policy, claims via IEF
Apps registered in the B2C tenant
Apps registered in the workforce tenant

A B2C tenant is created through the Azure Portal. The tenant name becomes the default domain (e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com) and the issuer URL for tokens. You choose a geographic region at creation time — it cannot be changed later.
In the Azure Portal, search for 'Azure AD B2C' and click Create.
Select 'Create a new Azure AD B2C Tenant'.
Enter an organization name and initial domain name (the .onmicrosoft.com suffix is automatic).
Choose the country/region — this determines data residency.
Select the Azure subscription and resource group for billing linkage.

# Create a B2C tenant — not available via az ad tenant; use az rest with the ARM API
# This creates the underlying AAD tenant and then converts it to B2C

# Step 1: Create an AAD tenant
az rest --method POST \
  --uri 'https://management.azure.com/tenants?api-version=2020-01-01' \
  --body '{
    "displayName": "Contoso B2C",
    "verifiedDomains": [{ "name": "contoso.onmicrosoft.com" }]
  }'

# Step 2: Link to subscription for billing
az resource create \
  --resource-group rg-b2c-prod \
  --resource-type Microsoft.AzureActiveDirectory/b2cDirectories \
  --name contoso.onmicrosoft.com \
  --location 'United States' \
  --properties '{
    "createTenantProperties": {
      "displayName": "Contoso B2C",
      "countryCode": "US"
    }
  }'
Linking a B2C Tenant to an Azure Subscription
Billing for B2C is handled through an Azure subscription. Linking is required before you can use the tenant in production. MAU (Monthly Active Users) beyond 50,000 are charged monthly.
Navigate to the B2C tenant in the portal (switch directories first).
Go to Azure AD B2C → Manage → Linked subscription.
Click Link an existing subscription and choose your billing subscription and resource group.
The billing meter appears under the chosen resource group as 'Azure Active Directory B2C'.
Resets monthly; free tier is per tenant, not per app
A user authenticating at least once per month counts as one MAU
Charged per MFA verification, not per user per month
Only when Identity Experience Framework policies are used
After creation, configure the tenant's core settings before registering applications or creating user flows.

# Switch context to the B2C tenant
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Organization.ReadWrite.All'

# Read current organization settings
$org = Get-MgOrganization
$org | Select-Object DisplayName, Id, CountryLetterCode, PreferredLanguage

# Update display name and preferred language
Update-MgOrganization -OrganizationId $org.Id -BodyParameter @{
    DisplayName       = 'Contoso Consumer Portal'
    PreferredLanguage = 'en-US'
    TechnicalNotificationMails = @('b2c-admin@contoso.com')
}
Write-Host 'Tenant settings updated.'
App Registrations in a B2C Tenant
Every application that uses the B2C tenant for authentication must be registered in that tenant. App registrations are isolated within the B2C directory — they do not appear in your workforce tenant.
Monitoring Tenant Health with Audit Logs

// B2C sign-in success and failure summary — last 7 days
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize
    TotalSignIns = count(),
    Successes = countif(ResultType == 0),
    Failures = countif(ResultType != 0)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), AppDisplayName
| extend SuccessRate = round(100.0 * Successes / TotalSignIns, 1)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Category in ('Policy', 'Application Management', 'UserManagement')
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    Category,
    OperationName,
    Actor = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName,
    Target = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
    Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
A B2C tenant cannot be merged into or converted to a workforce tenant.
Azure services (VMs, storage, Key Vault) cannot be deployed into a B2C tenant — it is an identity-only directory.
B2C does not support Microsoft 365 applications or Intune enrollment.
Maximum of one B2C tenant per Azure subscription (recommendation; not a hard limit).
The tenant region is permanent — data residency cannot be changed after creation.
B2C is not available in sovereign clouds (US Government, China) in all regions.
Custom domains require manual CNAME verification and cannot use the .onmicrosoft.com domain for end-user URLs.
The azurerm_aadb2c_directory resource provisions and links a B2C tenant to a subscription in a single Terraform resource block. This enables repeatable tenant creation across environments.

B2C uses Azure AD role assignments within the tenant to control who can manage policies, app registrations, and user flows. At minimum you need a Global Administrator or B2C IEF Policy Administrator for custom policy work.
Upload and manage custom XML policies
External Identity User Flow Attribute Administrator
Create, read, update, delete consumer users

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contosociam.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory'

# Get the 'B2C IEF Policy Administrator' role definition
$roleDefId = (Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleDefinition |
    Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -eq 'B2C IEF Policy Administrator' }).Id

# Get the target user
$userId = (Get-MgUser -Filter "userPrincipalName eq 'dev@contosociam.onmicrosoft.com'").Id

# Assign the role
New-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleAssignment -BodyParameter @{
    RoleDefinitionId = $roleDefId
    PrincipalId      = $userId
    DirectoryScopeId = '/'
}
Write-Host 'Role assigned successfully.'

B2C uses signing keys (RSA key sets) to sign the tokens it issues. Keys are managed within the tenant and should be rotated periodically. Custom policies (IEF) use named key sets referenced in XML policy files.
Creating and Rotating Signing Keys via Graph
Tenant deletion is permanent and irreversible. All user accounts, app registrations, policies, and branding are destroyed. There is no soft-delete or recycle bin.
Before deleting, you must remove all app registrations, enterprise applications, and unlink the subscription. Deletion is blocked if any of these prerequisites are outstanding.

# Pre-deletion checklist — run in the B2C tenant context
Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contosociam.onmicrosoft.com' `
    -Scopes 'Application.ReadWrite.All','Directory.ReadWrite.All'

# 1. List remaining app registrations
$apps = Get-MgApplication
Write-Host "Apps to remove: $($apps.Count)"
foreach ($app in $apps) {
    Remove-MgApplication -ApplicationId $app.Id
    Write-Host "Deleted: $($app.DisplayName)"
}

# 2. List enterprise applications (service principals)
$sps = Get-MgServicePrincipal | Where-Object { $_.Tags -notcontains 'WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryIntegratedApp' }
foreach ($sp in $sps) {
    Remove-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId $sp.Id -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}

# 3. After prerequisites met, delete tenant via Azure Portal:
# Azure AD B2C -> Manage -> Properties -> Delete directory
Write-Host 'Pre-deletion cleanup complete. Proceed via portal to delete the directory.'
Azure AD B2C does not offer a native point-in-time restore. User data, custom policies, and app registrations should be backed up externally as part of a disaster recovery strategy.

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contosociam.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'User.ReadBasic.All'

# Export all B2C users with key attributes to a JSON file
$users = Get-MgUser -All -Property 'id,displayName,mail,identities,createdDateTime,accountEnabled'
$export = $users | Select-Object Id, DisplayName, Mail, Identities, CreatedDateTime, AccountEnabled
$export | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Out-File './b2c-users-backup.json' -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "Exported $($users.Count) users to b2c-users-backup.json"

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contosociam.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Policy.Read.All'

# Export all IEF custom policies as XML files
$policies = Get-MgTrustFrameworkPolicy
foreach ($policy in $policies) {
    $xml = Get-MgTrustFrameworkPolicyContent -TrustFrameworkPolicyId $policy.Id
    $xml | Out-File "./policies/$($policy.Id).xml" -Encoding UTF8
    Write-Host "Backed up: $($policy.Id)"
}
Write-Host 'Policy backup complete.'
An Azure AD B2C tenant is a separate, isolated Azure AD directory purpose-built for consumer identity — distinct from the workforce tenant.
Billing is based on Monthly Active Users (MAU) with a 50,000 MAU free tier per tenant per month.
Tenants are created through the Azure Portal or ARM API and must be linked to an Azure subscription for billing.
All applications must be registered in the B2C tenant; app registrations are not shared with the workforce directory.
Token lifetime policies, organizational branding, and audit logs are configured per-tenant via the Microsoft Graph API.
Azure AD B2C is the legacy CIAM platform; Microsoft Entra External ID is the recommended choice for new projects.
